title: cynoglossum lanceolatum var. mannii
intro: Cynoglossum lanceolatum var. mannii, also known as Mann's Hound's Tongue, is a perennial plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to East Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, helping to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Mann's Hound's Tongue include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
